items.title: PM 'confident' about Whyalla amid work to bring furnace 'back to life'

children.content: Anthony Albanese says he remains "very confident" in the future of steelmaking in Whyalla, and says the federal government is "continuing to work" with lead producer Nyrstar to secure its operations in Port Pirie and Hobart.

gallery.altText: A view of the Whyalla steelworks.

thumbnail.altText: A view of the Whyalla steelworks.

items.title: Students of newly merged Adelaide University protest changing of graduation venue

children.content: The newly merged Adelaide University has changed the venue of its graduation ceremonies from Bonython Hall, to Pridham Hall with students launching a petition in opposition.

image.altText: Bonython Hall - an old heritage brick building in the Adelaide CBD

items.title: Steel and lead are facing trial by fire in SA's regional cities

children.content: The South Australian government has put all its chips on the table for the Whyalla steelworks and Port Pirie lead smelter. The next six months will be critical.

image.altText: A worker at the face of the blast furnace at the Whyalla steelworks.

items.title: Elk have been spotted in the wild in SA. Where did they come from?

children.content: A South Australian landowner has spotted elk on her property, outside containment lines of deer farms. There are no known established wild elk herds in this state.

gallery.altText: two large elk stand next to bushland.

thumbnail.altText: two large elk stand next to bushland.

items.title: Stronger bait for farmers approved amid mouse plague

children.content: Farm groups have welcomed the chemical regulator's long-awaited decision to approve double-strength mouse baits amid plague numbers in Western Australia and South Australia.

image.altText: Three mice in farm shed.

items.title: First Nations art collective calls for industry inquiry to protect artists

children.content: APY Art Centre Collective members say a voluntary code of conduct isn't protecting artists from financial exploitation and misconduct in the industry.

image.altText: The exterior of a building with corrugated metal sheets and a bright red logo, reading APY Art Centre Collective

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: Welcome to campdraft school, where a new generation takes the reins

children.content: Amid the red dirt and flies of an outback stockyard, thrillseekers with a passion for horses learn about a quintessential Aussie sport.

image.altText: a cow running away from a girl riding a horse on red dirt with dust

items.title: Protesters march at Nakba Day rallies around Australia

children.content: Crowds gather at rallies around the country to commemorate the anniversary of the Nakba, a day that recognises the mass displacement of Palestinians in 1948.

image.altText: A agthering of people on a city street with some holding Palestinian flags.

items.title: Fracking ban rethink on back-burner — but is it delaying the inevitable?

children.content: The SA government argues South East fracking would help unlock gas reserves at a time of limited supply — but it's a move that's vehemently opposed by many in the local community.

gallery.altText: Three men holding yellow signs with anti-fracking and gas messages on them

thumbnail.altText: Three men holding yellow signs with anti-fracking and gas messages on them

items.title: Australians with disabilities on love, lust and the right to intimacy

children.content: It's a stormy February night in Adelaide, and Jacqueline Tedmanson is at the hospital with her partner Jackson, waiting to meet their first baby.

image.altText: A female burlesque performer poses confidently in a wheelchair. 5 months pregnant, she is in a rehearsal room.

items.title: Stolen car involved in alleged shooting spotted at SA prison staff car park days earlier

children.content: The Public Service Association is calling for urgent and immediate security upgrades after a stolen car allegedly involved in a shooting was seen at the Yatala Labour Prison in the days before the incident.

image.altText: Nissan SUV

items.title: A house fire cost Patrick and Susan everything, including their two dogs

children.content: Patrick and Susan Doyle recently lost almost everything in a house fire, including their beloved dogs, Oscar and Mercy. While the couple escaped with their lives, Mrs Doyle was badly burnt. They are warning others to be better prepared.

image.altText: Two dogs standing in front of a car

items.title: Miles declares 'a win is a win' as Labor squeaks by in by-election

children.content: Labor candidate Luke Richmond will become the next member for Stafford, but the party's vote dropped several points.

image.altText: A man in a white buttoned shirt standing in front of press microphones with trees in the background.

avatar.alt: ABC Queensland state political reporter Jack McKay

items.title: A Canberra woman's husband said not to book return flights. It was a case of exit trafficking

children.content: Advocates for migrant women say they're seeing more cases of exit trafficking — where an offender uses coercion, threats or deception to organise a person's departure from Australia.

gallery.altText: A clip art image of a woman, passport and cancelled stamp

thumbnail.altText: A clip art image of a woman, passport and cancelled stamp

items.title: Church warns 'everything's on the table' as redress bill continues to climb

children.content: Tasmania's Anglican Church says what it expects to pay in compensation to victim-survivors of child sexual abuse has more than doubled in the past three years.

image.altText: A bishop standing in a church, looking at the camera.

items.title: Taiwan president responds after Trump's visit to China

children.content: Speaking after Donald Trump's meeting with China's president, Taiwan's leader says the island will not be sacrificed or traded and will not give up its free way of life.

image.altText: man smiles with green backdrop.

items.title: Why the US, UK and NZ haven't raised interest rates in years

children.content: As Australians pay more on their mortgages, some countries have had no rise in their interest rate in recent years. So what's going on?

gallery.altText: A for sale sign sits outside a two-storey property with a white picket fence

thumbnail.altText: A for sale sign sits outside a two-storey property with a white picket fence

items.title: What to know about the current Ebola outbreak in Uganda and the Congo

children.content: The WHO has declared a global health emergency after more than 80 cases of Ebola were confirmed across two African countries. Here's why the organisation is calling for preparedness, not panic.

image.altText: A man arrives at Bunia General Referral Hospital following confirmation of an Ebola outbreak

items.title: ASX falls after Wall Street losses, AUD also lower, oil up sharply

children.content: The ASX 200 has begun the week in the red, following Wall Street, with both US indexes suffering losses after touching record highs in their last trading sessions. Meanwhile, uncertainty over the Iran war has seen Brent crude oil rise above $111 a barrel. Follow live.

image.altText: A woman is seen in the reflection of a window looking at shares' performance on a board of the ASX

items.title: Young woman killed, three injured in northern NSW crash

children.content: Police are investigating after a car flipped after crashing into a power pole.

image.altText: Images of blue and white checkered police tape stretched across a suburban street.

items.title: Some NT cases could halt if landmark legal challenge succeeds

children.content: Lawyers in the Northern Territory are commencing a landmark legal challenge that could require courts to halt some cases due to defendants being unable to access a lawyer amid a worsening legal aid crisis.

image.altText: A man with a serious expression sits on a long wooden table with legal books behind him.

items.title: The $10b offer Dave Hughes would 'hand on heart' turn down

children.content: Comedian Dave Hughes spoke to The Assembly about why he quit alcohol at 22, being "addicted to work" and the type of dad he's trying to be for his three kids.

gallery.altText: Dave Hughes holds a microphone and is lit with stage lights.

thumbnail.altText: Dave Hughes holds a microphone and is lit with stage lights.

avatar.alt: Woman with neat, short hair smiles warmly for professional head portrait. Blurred trees are in the background

items.title: How an Australian designer's fashion fame spurred a mental health unravelling

children.content: Rochelle Gregory has clothed everyone from Paris Hilton to Dua Lipa. Now the designer is using film to candidly explore her mental health battle while showcasing her latest collection.

image.altText: A woman in a black leather jacket struts down a runway while women clap.

items.title: Reasons it's so hard to end a long-term relationship, even when you want to

children.content: It's not just practical and emotional reasons that get in the way of executing a break-up. There are many ways your brain is making you want to stay.

image.altText: woman looking sad with man blurred in the background

items.title: Qld developer donation overhaul yields $170,000 in cash

children.content: More than $170,000 in donations from developers has been given to Queensland state politicians since laws banning developer donations were overturned in March.

gallery.altText: Jarrod Bleijie stands in front of an easel holding plans for a development.

thumbnail.altText: Jarrod Bleijie stands in front of an easel holding plans for a development.

items.title: 'Make it legal next time': Another blistering 100m for Aussie Nketia

children.content: For the second time this year, Eddie Nketia stops the clock in what would be an Australian record for the 100m, but is aided by an illegal tailwind.

image.altText: Eddie Nketia yells to celebrate winning the 100m for USC.

items.title: The gas that could make Australia a player in global semiconductor supply chain

children.content: Helium is used in health care and to make microchips and semiconductors, but the Middle East war has affected supply. Some think it is an opportunity for Australia's gas industry.

image.altText: A helium well in a grain field

items.title: Mass showers and squalor: What spurred ex-Labor minister's NDIS fight

children.content: A former NSW Labor powerbroker and state disability minister pivotal in advocating for the establishment of the NDIS has warned against rushing through sweeping changes to the scheme.

gallery.altText: A middle aged man with short grey hair sitting on a verandah

thumbnail.altText: A middle aged man with short grey hair sitting on a verandah

items.title: Murder accused to remain in custody after man's death north of Bundaberg

children.content: Yutthawichan Phetrchaisak is remanded in custody after being charged with the murder of a 42-year-old man north of Bundaberg.

image.altText: A police officer walking past an open shed door with police tape

items.title: Person dies as fire in north Canberra unit causes 'substantial damage'

children.content: Emergency services were called to the Wallcliffe Flats in Belconnen at about 6:30am where they found a ground-floor unit alight.

image.altText: A fire truck and personnel.

items.title: Pilots parachute from jets after mid-flight collision at US air show

children.content: Two fighter jets have collided mid-air during an air show at a US Air Force base, forcing pilots to eject and parachute to safety.

image.altText: An E/A-18G Growler launches from the flight deck of the USS Ronald Reagan.

items.title: Tax is a great motivator but will Labor's changes fix Australia's housing mess?

children.content: The Albanese government and the opposition both want to turn back the clock on the Australian tax system.

image.altText: Albanese is in focus as he sits on the green bench watching Chalmers speak
